Advice requested on January 14, 1982, by Mr. John Porter 
(714) 682-2881, city attorney of Lake Elsinore in 
Riverside County, concerning an official decision which has 
already been made. 
Submitted to Opinion Request Meeting 
January 14, 1982 (copy of opinion request attached). 
Mr. 
Porter's address is: 
John M. Porter, Roberts, Morgan & 
Dougherty, 5015 Canyon Crest Drive, Riverside, CA 92507 
Issue #1: 
Realty Center 
(actual name: Realty Brokerage Center 
is a source of income to Escrow Center (actual name: 
Realty Escrow 
Center). 
As both entities are partnerships controlled by the same 
partners, the individual partners as well as the Escrow Center part-
nership are the actual sources of the planning commissioner's 
income, especially where, as here, the partners exercise day-to-day 
management and control over the ooerations of both partnerships and 
both partnerships are located within the sa~me facility. 
A financi 
effect on one partnership under these circumstances would be tanta-
mount to an effect on the other. 
(See letter of December or 
November 1980 regarding solely owned corporation.) 
Issue #2: 
Yes, where the public official knows that the 
business partners own both entities, and where the partners exercise 
day to day control over both operations within the same facility. 
{This issue was not thoroughly addressed.) 
Issue #3: 
No. 
"Legally required participation" does not 
apply in a situation where a quor~~ simply fails to show up. 
The 
matter must be postponed. 


State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
From 
Subject: 
Facts: 
Opinion Request Meeting participants 
FAIR POLlTI~AL ~CTICES COMMISSION 
Judy DaV1S (JJP 
Dote 
: Jan. 14, 19 8 2 
Attribution of material financial effect on one partnership 
to another partnership (having the same partners) which is 
a source of income to a public official 
A planning commissioner works for a partnership known as 
Escrow Center. All she handles is escrows, and she is paid on a 
commission basis. 
Escrow Center is a source of income to her. 
Realty Center, a partnership owned by the same partners as own 
Escrow Center, operates out of the same location as Escrow Center. 
Realty Center is the broker on the sale of a certain parcel of 
property and will receive between $5,000-$10,000 commission on 
the sale. 
The escrow is not being handled by Escrow Center or any 
company related to it. 
The public official knows that Realty 
Center is involved in this sale but knows none of the details. 
Once she found out that another escrow company was handling the 
escrow and that she would not be involved, she assumed she had no 
conflict of interest. 
The sale of this property mayor may not ~e" 
(but probably is) contingent upon the dedication of certain frontage 
in connection with the buyer's plan to develop the property. 
As 
the commission on the sale will amount to 1% of Realty Center's 
gross receipts for the year and is over $1,000, Realty Center will 
be materially affected by the decision if the sale is contingent 
upon the decision. 
The planning commissioner votes to approve the frontage dedi-
cation but recommends that construction of a block wall along the 
frontage be a condition attached to the dedication. 
On the evening 
of the vote, only three of the five planning commissioners are 
present. 
The vote is 3-0. 
l. 
virtue of 
there be 
/ 
/ 
Is Realty Center a source of income to Escrow Center by 
the same individuals being partners in both? 
Or must 
some further connection, such as commingled funds, etc.? 
2. 
Where the public official knows nothing about the finan-
cial interaction between the two partnerships, but knows only that 
the same partners own both, is she in possession of enough informa-
tion to make it reasonably foreseeable to her that a financial 
effect on one will be tantamount to a financial effect on the other? 


Opinion Request Meeting 
January 14, 1982 
Page Two 
3. 
If it was reasonably foreseeable to the public official 
at the time she made the decision that the decision would have an 
attributable material financial effect on her source of income, 
was her participation nevertheless legally required? 
(She 
obviously didn't comply with 18701, but then she didn't believe 
she had a conflict.) 
JD:sf

sections.question: 1. Is Realty Center a source of income to Escrow Center by virtue of the same individuals being partners in both? Or must there be some further connection, such as commingled funds, etc.? 2. Where the public official knows nothing about the financial interaction between the two partnerships, but knows only that the same partners own both, is she in possession of enough information to make it reasonably foreseeable to her that a financial effect on one will be tantamount to a financial effect on the other? 3. If it was reasonably foreseeable to the public official at the time she made the decision that the decision would have an attributable material financial effect on her source of income, was her participation nevertheless legally required? (She obviously didn't comply with 18701, but then she didn't believe she had a conflict.)
sections.conclusion: Issue #1: Realty Center (actual name: Realty Brokerage Center) is a source of income to Escrow Center (actual name: Realty Escrow Center). As both entities are partnerships controlled by the same partners, the individual partners as well as the Escrow Center partnership are the actual sources of the planning commissioner's income, especially where, as here, the partners exercise day-to-day management and control over the operations of both partnerships and both partnerships are located within the same facility. A financial effect on one partnership under these circumstances would be tantamount to an effect on the other. Issue #2: Yes, where the public official knows that the business partners own both entities, and where the partners exercise day to day control over both operations within the same facility. (This issue was not thoroughly addressed.) Issue #3: No. "Legally required participation" does not apply in a situation where a quorum simply fails to show up. The matter must be postponed.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asks whether a planning commissioner who has a financial interest in one partnership (Escrow Center) has a disqualifying conflict when voting on a matter affecting another partnership (Realty Center) owned by the same partners, and whether such a conflict was reasonably foreseeable or legally required to disclose.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that a material financial effect on one partnership is attributable to the other partnership when the same partners control both and operate them jointly. A public official with knowledge that the same partners own both entities can reasonably foresee a financial connection between them. However, "legally required participation" does not excuse a conflict of interest when a quorum fails to appear; the matter must be postponed instead.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the initial advice request and the opinion request meeting memoranda. The conclusion sections are somewhat fragmented across multiple issues but have been consolidated. Document ID appears as GC-82-39 in header. Some text quality issues due to document age/scanning (e.g., 'ooerations' instead of 'operations'). Issue #2 conclusion notes it was not thoroughly addressed.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Is Realty Center a source of income to Escrow Center by virtue of the same individuals being partners in both? Or must there be some further connection, such as commingled funds, etc.? 2. Where the public official knows nothing about the financial interaction between the two partnerships, but knows only that the same partners own both, is she in possession of enough information to make it reasonably foreseeable to her that a financial effect on one will be tantamount to a financial effect on the other? 3. If it was reasonably foreseeable to the public official at the time she made the decision that the decision would have an attributable material financial effect on her source of income, was her participation nevertheless legally required? (She obviously didn't comply with 18701, but then she didn't believe she had a conflict.)

CONCLUSION: Issue #1: Realty Center (actual name: Realty Brokerage Center) is a source of income to Escrow Center (actual name: Realty Escrow Center). As both entities are partnerships controlled by the same partners, the individual partners as well as the Escrow Center partnership are the actual sources of the planning commissioner's income, especially where, as here, the partners exercise day-to-day management and control over the operations of both partnerships and both partnerships are located within the same facility. A financial effect on one partnership under these circumstances would be tantamount to an effect on the other. Issue #2: Yes, where the public official knows that the business partners own both entities, and where the partners exercise day to day control over both operations within the same facility. (This issue was not thoroughly addressed.) Issue #3: No. "Legally required participation" does not apply in a situation where a quorum simply fails to show up. The matter must be postponed.

embedding.summary: An FPPC advice letter responding to a planning commissioner's inquiry about whether her financial interest in Escrow Center creates a conflict of interest regarding a vote on a property sale where Realty Center (owned by the same partners) would receive a commission. The FPPC concludes that the commissioner should have recognized the conflict and that the matter should have been postponed rather than voted on with an incomplete quorum.
